LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED AS TO THE FIRST, SECOND, AND SEVENTH CAUSE OF ACTION. THE COMPLAINT ADEQUATELY SETS FORTH THE ULTIMATE FACTS NECESSARY TO ESTABLISH EACH ELEMENT OF THE CAUSE OF ACTION. SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION: VIOLATION OF CIVIL CODE SECTION 1708.8. EACH CAUSE OF ACTION WITHIN THE COMPLAINT MUST PROPERLY IDENTIFY DEFENDANTS [SEE CALIFORNIA RULE OF COURT 2.112]. PREVAILING PARTY SHALL PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: MELANIE DAWN GHENO, CSR #7489; DEPT. 302. (302/EHG)

LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT ISMAEL BASCO'S MOTION TO STRIKE PLAINTIFFS' CLAIMS FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES AND ATTORNEY'S FEES IN PLAINTIFFS' COMPLAINT ARE DENIED. PLAINTIFFS' ALLEGATIONS OF ULTIMATE FACTS ARE SUFFICIENT WITH REGARD TO THE PRAYER FOR RELIEF [SEE CCP 452; PH II, INC. V. SUPERIOR COURT, 33 CAL. APP. 4TH 1680, 1682]. PREVAILING PARTY SHALL PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: MELANIE DAWN GHENO, CSR #7489; DEPT. 302. (302/EHG)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-27-2015 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. As to the first, second, and seventh Cause of Action, DEFENDANT ISMAEL BASCO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is overruled. The Complaint adequately sets forth the ultimate facts necessary to establish each element of the cause of action. Sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to the third cause of action: Violation of Civil Code Section 1708.8. Each Cause of Action within the Complaint must properly identify Defendants [See California Rule of Court 2.112]. PREVAILING PARTY SHALL PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Dawn Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-27-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT ISMAEL BASCO'S Motion to Strike Plaintiffs' Claims for Punitive Damages and Attorney's Fees in Plaintiffs' Complaint are denied. Plaintiffs' allegations of ultimate facts are sufficient with regard to the Prayer for Relief [See CCP 452; PH II, Inc. v. Superior Court, 33 Cal. App. 4th 1680, 1682]. PREVAILING PARTY SHALL PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Dawn Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)
